phrasalVerbs.definition: To find the total of several numbers or amounts.
examples: Can you add up the prices for me?
I added up the costs before buying anything.
She added up the points in her notebook.
Luckily, the old gas canister didn't blow up.
The fireworks blew up in the sky.
The bomb disposal team made sure nothing would blow up.

phrasalVerbs.definition: If a machine or vehicle breaks down, it stops working.
examples: Our car broke down on the motorway.
My laptop broke down during the presentation.
The lift broke down again this morning.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To enter a building illegally, usually by force.
examples: Someone tried to break in last night.
Thieves broke into the office at dawn.
They called the police after someone broke in.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To take care of a child until they become an adult.
examples: She brought up three children on her own.
They were brought up in a small town.
His grandparents helped bring him up.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To increase or develop something gradually.
examples: You need to build up your confidence.
He built up his business over many years.
These exercises build up leg strength.
I'll call you back after my meeting.
She called me back within five minutes.
Can you call back tomorrow morning?

phrasalVerbs.definition: To cancel an event or plan.
examples: They called off the concert because of the weather.
We had to call off the match.
The meeting was called off at the last minute.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To become calmer or to make someone calmer.
examples: Please calm down and tell me what happened.
She finally calmed down after the argument.
Music helps me calm down.

fillInExercises.sentence: Can you ___ ___ these numbers before we pay?
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Add up' means to calculate the total.

fillInExercises.sentence: The old van ___ down on the way to the beach.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Break down' means to stop working (for machines/vehicles).

fillInExercises.sentence: Someone tried to ___ ___ our flat last night.
She ___ up four children while working full-time.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Bring up' means to raise a child.

fillInExercises.sentence: I'll ___ you ___ when I finish this call.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Call back' means to phone again later.

fillInExercises.sentence: They had to ___ off the picnic because it started raining.
Regular training helps athletes ___ up strength.

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.text: I had a terrible start today—my car [broke down|break down] on the ring road.

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Oh no. Did you [call back|call back] your mechanic?

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.text: Yes, and while I waited I tried to [calm down|calm down].

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Good idea. Last week someone tried to [break into|break in (to)] our building.

lines.speaker: Lena
lines.text: That's awful. By the way, can you [add up|add up] these receipts for me?

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.text: Sure. Also, the afternoon meeting was [called off|call off], so we can leave early.

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: My sister [brought up|bring up] her kids on her own.

lines.speaker: Rafa
lines.text: She's amazing. She also [built up|build up] her own business, right?

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Exactly. Oh, I have to go now, but I'll [call back|call back] later.

lines.speaker: Rafa
lines.text: No problem. Don't [blow up|blow up] my phone with messages this time!

lines.speaker: Marta
lines.text: Haha, I won't. Just [calm down|calm down].

lines.speaker: Rafa
lines.text: Deal. Let's hope they don't [call off|call off] the weekend festival.
